questions.optionB: Clean wound edges are approximated (sutured) — minimal scarring, e.g., surgical incision closed primarily
questions.explanation: Primary intention (first intention): clean wound with edges approximated (sutured/stapled/taped). Minimal granulation tissue, minimal scarring, fastest healing. Example: clean surgical incision. Secondary intention: wound left open → granulation tissue fills defect → wound contracts → epithelializes. More scarring. Example: abscess cavity, open wound. Tertiary intention (delayed primary closure): wound left open initially (contaminated), then closed later after infection controlled. Reference: Bailey & Love — Wound Healing.

questions.questionText: The predominant cell type during the proliferative phase of wound healing is:
questions.optionB: Fibroblasts (synthesize collagen — Type III initially, replaced by Type I)
questions.explanation: Wound healing phases: (1) Hemostasis (immediate): platelet plug, fibrin clot. (2) Inflammatory (days 1-4): neutrophils first (24-48h, clear debris), then macrophages (48-72h — most important cell, orchestrate healing). (3) Proliferative (days 4-21): fibroblasts predominate → synthesize collagen (Type III initially → replaced by Type I during remodeling), angiogenesis (new blood vessels), granulation tissue formation, epithelialization, wound contraction (myofibroblasts). (4) Remodeling (3 weeks-2 years): collagen remodeling, maximal wound strength ~80% of original. Reference: Bailey & Love — Wound Healing Phases.

questions.questionText: Keloid differs from hypertrophic scar in that keloid:
questions.optionB: Extends BEYOND original wound boundaries, does NOT regress spontaneously, and has higher recurrence after excision
questions.explanation: Keloid: excessive scar that extends BEYOND original wound margins. Does NOT regress spontaneously. High recurrence after excision. More common in: dark skin, earlobes, sternum, deltoid, upper back. Excess Type III collagen. Treatment: intralesional steroid (triamcinolone), silicone sheets, pressure therapy, radiation, excision + adjuvant therapy. Hypertrophic scar: stays WITHIN wound boundaries, regresses with time, lower recurrence. Both: excess collagen deposition. Reference: Bailey & Love — Keloid vs Hypertrophic Scar.

questions.questionText: The most important cell in wound healing orchestration is:
questions.optionB: Macrophage (coordinates all phases — debridement, growth factor release, angiogenesis, fibroblast recruitment)
questions.explanation: Macrophage: most important cell in wound healing. Arrives 48-72h (monocyte → macrophage). Functions: phagocytosis/debridement, release growth factors (PDGF, TGF-β, FGF, VEGF), recruit fibroblasts and endothelial cells, promote angiogenesis, collagen synthesis, and extracellular matrix remodeling. Without macrophages: wound healing is severely impaired. Neutrophils: first responders but not essential for healing (depletion doesn't impair healing in clean wounds). Reference: Bailey & Love — Macrophage in Healing.
